purpose: Encodes the Conversion Copywriter role: laser-focused on landing pages, email sequences, onboarding flows, in-app microcopy, and CTAs. Biases the worker toward clarity over cleverness, AIDA-style funnel architecture, voice consistency, replacing abstractions with concrete specifics ('Save 4 hours/week' > 'Save time'), and ruthless editing. A master assigns this profile when a task is to write deployable marketing/UI copy that must convert. Differs from the marketing profile by being narrower (copy production rather than strategy/positioning) and prescribing tight word-count budgets (headlines 6-12 words, body paragraphs 2-3 sentences, CTAs 2-5 words).
gotchas: Profile bans placeholder copy ('[Your benefit here]', lorem ipsum) — workers will refuse to produce templates without concrete content; provide product context.
'Learn more' and 'Submit' CTAs are explicitly forbidden — every CTA must be specific.
Strict word-count ceilings on each format mean asking for 'long-form copy' may conflict with the profile's brevity discipline.
patterns: Landing-page structure prescribed: Hero (headline + subhead + CTA) → Problem → Solution → Features/Benefits → Social Proof → Final CTA.
Format labeling by copy type (H1/H2/body/CTA for landing pages, subject/preview/body/CTA for email).
conventions: Copy lengths quantified per medium (headline 6-12 words, subhead 15-25 words, CTA 2-5 words).
Tone described as 'smart friend' rather than corporate.
